perturbation.original_text: “Confidential information” means any information, electronic data and/or documents relating to the Protectable Interests which are not readily available to a competitor of the parties in the ordinary and regular c ourse of business;
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential information” means any information, electronic data and/or documents relating to the Protectable Interests, regardless of its availability to competitors or whether it is obtained in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.explanation: This modification broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information that might be readily available to competitors, contradicting the standard legal understanding of trade secrets and confidential information, which typically requires an element of secrecy and non-public availability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: South African common law principles relating to trade secrets and confidential information, specifically the requirement of secrecy.
perturbation.law_citation: Case law on trade secrets in South Africa (e.g., Waste Products Utilisation (Pty) Ltd v Wilkes 2003 (2) SA 515 (W))
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text eliminates the requirement that confidential information must *not* be readily available to competitors. South African law requires that trade secrets maintain some level of secrecy to be legally protected. By removing this requirement, the contract attempts to protect information that would otherwise not qualify, creating a conflict with established legal principles.

perturbation.justification: The modified text broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information readily available to competitors, negating the element of secrecy typically required for trade secret protection. The Law Explanation states that South African law requires trade secrets to maintain some level of secrecy to be legally protected. By eliminating the requirement that the information not be readily available, the contract conflicts with established legal principles.

perturbation.original_text: Should either party (“the selling party”) sell their b usiness or any part thereof, then the other party hereby agrees that the selling party shall be entitled to cede the transfer to the purchaser of the business all of the selling party’s right , title and interest in and to this agreement and that the purcha ser shall be entitled to enforce the terms of the agreement against the other party as if the purchaser were a signatory to this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Should either party (“the selling party”) sell their business or any part thereof, then the selling party shall automatically transfer this agreement to the purchaser, without requiring consent from the other party. The purchaser may enforce the terms of this agreement regardless of the original intent.
perturbation.explanation: This modification attempts to automatically transfer the agreement without consent, potentially conflicting with South African law regarding cession and the requirement for good faith in contractual relationships. It also makes the enforcement by the purchaser absolute, disregarding potential legal limitations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: South African law of Cession and Contract Law principles related to consent and good faith (bona fides).
perturbation.law_explanation: South African law requires that there is a consensus of both the cedent and the cessionary for a cession to be legally valid. By indicating an automatic transfer, the modified text could be interpreted as attempting to circumvent the requirement for consent from the non-selling party, which may not be permissible depending on the specific context and wording of the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Welcome to LII | Legal Information Institute Skip to main content Welcome to LII We believe that everyone should be able to read and understand the laws that govern them, without cost. We carry out this vision by: Publishing law online, for free. Creating materials that help people understand law. Exploring new technologies that make it easier for people to find the law. We hope you'll learn more about us, and help support our work. What's New? State Regulations LII now publishes state regulations for all 50 U.S. states . Updates are released quarterly. We're hiring! Keep an eye on this space for new position postings as they become available. LII Original Content The Wex Legal Dictionary and Encyclopedia has new entries on statutory interpretation , corruption , and summaries of recent U.S. Supreme Court decisions. The LII Supreme Court Bulletin students prepare and publish previews of upcoming oral arguments before the U.S. Supreme Court . Recent previews include: Trump v. Anderson The Gender Justice Collection now features a new set of resources on legislation in Ukraine . From the Government The Congressional Research Service's U.S. Constitution Annotated now features updates through the most recent U.S. Supreme Court decisions. Updates include: Modern Doctrine on Appropriate Scrutiny (college admissions) States and Elections Clause (redistricting) True Threats (social media) The U.S. Supreme Court collection : most recent opinions and Code of Conduct for Justices The U.S. Code is up to date through the most recent version published by the Office of the Law Revision Counsel; the CFR is up to date through the end of last week. Legal Resources Primary sources Constitution U.S. Code C.F.R. and Supreme Court Executive Orders Federal Rules State law and State regulations U.C.C. perturbation.justification: The modification in the changed text introduces an automatic transfer of the agreement without requiring consent from the other party. This contradicts the principle highlighted in the Law Explanation, which states that South African law requires consensus from both parties for a cession to be legally valid. The changed text makes the purchaser's enforcement absolute, disregarding legal limitations. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines Legal Information Institute's mission to make laws understandable, which underscores the need for contracts to align with legal principles, a principle the altered contract violates by potentially circumventing consent requirements.

perturbation.original_text: In the implementation of this agreement, the parties undertake to observe the utmost good faith and they warrant in their dealings with one anothe r that they shall neither do anything nor refrain from doing anything that might prejudice the rights, assets or interests of the other(s).
perturbation.changed_text: In the implementation of this agreement, the parties are encouraged to act in good faith; however, actions taken to maximize individual profit are permissible, even if they indirectly affect the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This clause weakens the obligation of good faith, potentially contradicting the implied duty of good faith inherent in South African contract law. Allowing actions that 'indirectly affect' the other party, even if those actions are for maximizing individual profit, undermines the principle of reciprocal fairness.
perturbation.contradicted_law: South African law principle of good faith (bona fides) in contract law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text attempts to permit actions that prioritize individual profit even if they prejudice the other party's interests. While the extent of the duty of good faith is debated, South African law generally recognizes a requirement to act honestly and reasonably in contractual dealings. The modification's allowance of actions that 'indirectly affect' the other party for profit conflicts with this expectation of fairness and reasonable conduct.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'utmost good faith' and prohibits actions that might prejudice the other party's interests. The changed text encourages good faith but permits actions maximizing individual profit, even if they 'indirectly affect' the other party. This creates a direct contradiction by allowing behavior that was previously prohibited, thus undermining the principle of reciprocal fairness inherent in contract law, as highlighted in the Perturbation and Law Explanations. While the provided snippets are not helpful, the explanation details the existence of a potential violation of good faith.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in acco rdance with South African law.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the principles of international commercial law, irrespective of any conflicting provisions in South African law.
perturbation.explanation: This clause attempts to override the application of South African law, which is the stated governing law. It introduces uncertainty by invoking 'international commercial law' which is a broad and often ill-defined body of principles. This creates a conflict and undermines the certainty afforded by choosing a specific jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of conflict of laws (private international law) and contractual choice of law.
perturbation.law_citation: Econet Wireless (Pty) Ltd v Vee Networks Ltd 2006 (2) SA 293 (A)
perturbation.law_explanation: By stating that 'international commercial law' prevails even when it contradicts South African law, the modified text creates ambiguity about which legal rules will actually govern the agreement. This contradicts the principle that the parties' choice of law should be respected and applied consistently, except in limited circumstances where it violates public policy or mandatory rules of another jurisdiction with a closer connection to the dispute.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: About | HeinOnline Law Journal Library | HeinOnline Law Journal Library | HeinOnline Please click here if you are not redirected within a few seconds. What Is HeinOnline? HeinOnline is a subscription-based resource containing thousands of academic and legal journals from inception; complete coverage of government documents such as U.S. Statutes at Large, U.S. Code, Federal Register, Code of Federal Regulations, U.S. Reports, and much more. Documents are image-based, fully searchable PDFs with the authority of print combined with the accessibility of a user-friendly and powerful database. For more information, request a quote or trial for your organization below. profiles profiles most
perturbation.justification: The changed text attempts to supplant South African law with 'international commercial law' even when the two conflict. The original text stipulated South African law. The perturbation explanation highlights that this undermines the certainty provided by choosing a specific jurisdiction. The LLM explanation states that this creates ambiguity. The scraped snippets do not provide additional support. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction as the contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable.
